66a0f6b2c4d8e90123456789 insuranceStart: You paid for speed. My people are already moving.
Volkov prices buy Volkov speed. Watch your messages.
66a0f6b2c4d8e90123456789 insuranceFound: Your gear is back. Fast work is not cheap, but you already knew that.
Recovered and stored. Take it before I start charging for the space.
66a0f6b2c4d8e90123456789 insuranceFailed: Nothing came back. Either someone kept it, or someone smarter than you found it first.
6a300025d2999bc7b9bba952 Description: An old security ledger from Concordia. It contains names, room numbers, camera notes, payoff records, and routes connected to Tony Volkov's people.
6a300025d2999bc7b9bba953 Description: A sealed envelope marked with one of Tony Volkov's old routing codes. The papers inside are folded tight and not meant for your eyes.
6a300025d2999bc7b9bba954 Description: A matte black card with no printed name. A faded wolf insignia sits in the center, cut by a single dark red stripe. In Tarkov, the right people would know exactly who left it.
6a30b2fbd2999bc7b9bbaaba Description: A sealed military weapon crate left in the Reserve E2 bunkers by one of Tony's people. The lock wire is intact, the markings are fresh, and the contents are not your concern.
6a3210dbd2999bc7b9bbab26 Description: A sealed medical case recovered from room W301 at the Health Resort. It contains standard injectors, rejected batches, and prototype stimulants packed under faded medical labels. Some markings are official. Some are handwritten. Tony only cares that the seals are intact.
6a3210dbd2999bc7b9bbab28 Description: A sealed field trauma bag recovered from Mantis on Interchange. It contains pressure bandages, clotting agents, surgical tools, painkillers, and a folded casualty tag marked with one of Tony's route codes.
6a300025d2999bc7b9bba906 description: You want Volkov to open his door? Then you do not come empty-handed. Tony likes proof, and I like insurance. Bring me a Roler first. After that, go to Ground Zero dressed like one of my people. Scav vest, Ushanka, cheap weapon, dirty work. Kill PMCs while they are still trying to figure out who you are. Then leave alive. If you can do that, Tony may decide you are worth hearing.
6a300025d2999bc7b9bba906 note: Fence wants you to pay for the introduction, then prove you can pass for Scav trash while killing PMCs on Ground Zero.
6a300025d2999bc7b9bba906 startedMessageT: Start with the watch. Then put on the scav kit and go to Ground Zero. Kill PMCs. Walk out alive.
6a300025d2999bc7b9bba906 successMessageT: Good. You paid the price, wore the rags, and still made PMCs bleed. Tony heard enough. The door is open, for now.
6a300025d2999bc7b9bba906 failMessageText: You made noise, but not the kind that opens doors.
6a300025d2999bc7b9bba906 completePlayerM: The watch is handed over. Ground Zero is handled.
6a300025d2999bc7b9bba906 changeQuestMess: Fence is watching how you handle the introduction.
6a300025d2999bc7b9bba907: Hand over 1 Roler Submariner gold wrist watch
6a300025d2999bc7b9bba908: Eliminate 8 PMCs on Ground Zero while wearing a Scav vest and Ushanka
6a300025d2999bc7b9bba909: Survive and extract from Ground Zero
6a300025d2999bc7b9bba916 description: Concordia had security before it had ghosts. Cameras, guards, records, keys, people who looked the other way when paid. One of my old ledgers used to sit in that security room, until someone smart moved it before the wrong eyes found it. Now it is stashed in a maintenance closet under the ramp on Ground Zero. Find it, recover it, and leave alive. Do not make a scene.
6a300025d2999bc7b9bba916 note: Tony wants you to recover an old security ledger hidden in a maintenance closet under the ramp on Ground Zero.
6a300025d2999bc7b9bba916 startedMessageT: Ground Zero. Maintenance closet under the ramp. My ledger is there now. Bring it back clean.
6a300025d2999bc7b9bba916 successMessageT: Good. That ledger in the wrong hands would have made problems. You followed instructions and kept your head down. That matters.
6a300025d2999bc7b9bba916 failMessageText: You either lost the trail or made too much noise following it.
6a300025d2999bc7b9bba916 declinePlayerMe: Not going into Ground Zero for that yet.
6a300025d2999bc7b9bba916 changeQuestMess: Tony wants the relocated ledger recovered quietly from Ground Zero.
6a300025d2999bc7b9bba917: Locate the maintenance closet under the ramp on Ground Zero
6a300025d2999bc7b9bba919: Survive and extract from Ground Zero
6a300025d2999bc7b9bba91b description: The Concordia ledger you recovered still has a useful name in it. One of my people left a sealed envelope in the Factory office building, second-floor locker room. You will move it to Ground Zero. Underground level, meat truck, far back. The man collecting it works for Prapor. He must not know where it came from or who carried it. Do not open it. Do not lose it. Do not bring it back to me.
6a300025d2999bc7b9bba91b note: Move Tony's sealed envelope from the second-floor Factory office locker room to the far back of the underground meat truck on Ground Zero.
6a300025d2999bc7b9bba91b startedMessageT: Factory office, second-floor locker room. Get the envelope. Then Ground Zero, underground meat truck, far back. Leave it there and walk away.
6a300025d2999bc7b9bba91b successMessageT: Good. The envelope is where it needs to be. Prapor's man will remember what he owes and who still has the paper to prove it.
6a300025d2999bc7b9bba91b failMessageText: You lost the envelope, lost the route, or lost your nerve.
6a300025d2999bc7b9bba91b changeQuestMess: Tony wants the sealed envelope moved through an old courier route.
6a300025d2999bc7b9bba91c: Locate the sealed envelope in the Factory office locker room
6a300025d2999bc7b9bba91f: Stash Tony's sealed envelope in the Ground Zero meat truck
6a300025d2999bc7b9bba920: Survive and extract from Ground Zero
6a300025d2999bc7b9bba921 description: That envelope reached the right hands. Funny thing about frightened men, they remember things. One of Prapor's people just remembered an old Reserve parts cache that never made it onto the books. Locked away behind RB-ST, where most rats stop looking. AK furniture, worn spares, magazines, cheap army scraps. I do not need you dragging metal across the base. Get inside, find the cache, mark it, and leave. My people will collect what Prapor forgot.
6a300025d2999bc7b9bba921 note: Tony wants you to access RB-ST on Reserve and mark a forgotten army parts cache so his people can recover it later.
6a300025d2999bc7b9bba921 startedMessageT: Go to Reserve. Get into RB-ST, find the old army parts cache, and mark it. Do not get greedy. Do not start opening crates.
6a300025d2999bc7b9bba921 successMessageT: Good. The cache is marked. Prapor's man gave us the memory, you got through the lock, and now the leftovers belong to me.
6a300025d2999bc7b9bba921 failMessageText: You either never got through RB-ST, missed the cache, or made too much noise near it. That is how useful things disappear.
6a300025d2999bc7b9bba921 acceptPlayerMes: I'll get into RB-ST and mark the cache.
6a300025d2999bc7b9bba921 declinePlayerMe: Not touching Prapor's locked leftovers yet.
6a300025d2999bc7b9bba921 changeQuestMess: Tony wants the RB-ST cache marked, not looted.
6a300025d2999bc7b9bba923: Locate the forgotten army parts cache behind RB-ST on Reserve
6a300025d2999bc7b9bba924: Mark the army parts cache for Tony
6a300025d2999bc7b9bba926 description: Names are dangerous. Symbols last longer. Take my card to Factory and leave it in the third-floor office, on the desk where people will see it. No signatures, no speeches, no bodies in Scav jackets. Fence opened the first door for you, and I do not repay useful men with dead workers. Plant the card, leave alive, and say nothing.
6a300025d2999bc7b9bba926 note: Tony wants you to plant his calling card on the third-floor office desk in Factory without killing Scavs.
6a300025d2999bc7b9bba926 startedMessageT: Factory. Third-floor office. Put the card on the desk. Do not kill Scavs.
6a300025d2999bc7b9bba926 successMessageT: Good. The card is on the desk, and the Scavs are still breathing. The right people will see the mark and understand without hearing my name.
6a300025d2999bc7b9bba926 failMessageText: You made noise where I asked for silence. A dead Scav carries the wrong message.
6a300025d2999bc7b9bba926 declinePlayerMe: Not going quiet into Factory yet.
6a300025d2999bc7b9bba926 changeQuestMess: Tony wants his calling card planted on the Factory third-floor office desk without Scav blood on it.
6a3007dfd2999bc7b9bba9b3: Locate the Third-Floor Office in Factory
6a300025d2999bc7b9bba927: Plant Tony's calling card on the third-floor office desk in Factory
6a300025d2999bc7b9bba929: Do not kill any Scavs on Factory
6a30a341d2999bc7b9bbaa32 description: The card was seen. Now comes the bill. Customs has roads for men who do not like checkpoints, offices for goods with no paperwork, dorm rooms where cash changes hands, and gas stations where deals get settled behind locked doors. Big Red keeps the paper. Three-story Dorms keeps the dirty money. New Gas keeps the route moving. Go there, collect what is owed, and leave alive. Bring me my cut. You keep the collector's fee.
6a30a341d2999bc7b9bbaa32 note: Tony wants you to collect valuables from Big Red, three-story Dorms, and New Gas on Customs, then bring him his cut.
6a30a341d2999bc7b9bbaa32 startedMessageT: Customs. Big Red upstairs office, three-story Dorms near marked room, then New Gas office. Collect the street tax and come back alive.
6a30a341d2999bc7b9bbaa32 successMessageT: Good. Big Red, Dorms, New Gas, cash, gold. The route remembers who owns it now. Keep your collector's fee. You earned that much.
6a30a341d2999bc7b9bbaa32 failMessageText: You lost the route, lost the money, or forgot whose tax you were collecting.
6a30a341d2999bc7b9bbaa32 changeQuestMess: Tony wants his cut collected from Big Red, Dorms, and New Gas.
6a30a341d2999bc7b9bbaa33: Locate the upstairs Big Red office collection point
6a30a341d2999bc7b9bbaa34: Locate the three-story Dorms marked room collection point
6a30a341d2999bc7b9bbaa35: Locate the New Gas office collection point
6a30b2fbd2999bc7b9bbaa96 description: The Customs route pays again. Good. But money is useless if every road is watched. Before Tarkov broke open, BEAR teams used paths through Woods that never touched the main roads. Old Station, Scav bunker, Abandoned Village. Those routes were quiet once. I want to know if they still are. Mark them, leave alive, and do not waste time looking for souvenirs. I am not sending you for supplies. I am seeing if you can follow ghosts.
6a30b2fbd2999bc7b9bbaa96 note: Tony wants you to mark three old BEAR route points on Woods and survive.
6a30b2fbd2999bc7b9bbaa96 startedMessageT: Woods. Old Station, Scav bunker, Abandoned Village. Mark the old BEAR routes and leave alive.
6a30b2fbd2999bc7b9bbaa96 successMessageT: Good. The old marks have new teeth. Those trails can still be walked, and now I know you can walk them.
6a30b2fbd2999bc7b9bbaa96 failMessageText: You lost the trail. That is how men disappear in Woods.
6a30b2fbd2999bc7b9bbaa96 declinePlayerMe: Not walking old BEAR trails yet.
6a30b2fbd2999bc7b9bbaa96 changeQuestMess: Tony wants the old BEAR routes marked on Woods.
6a30b2fbd2999bc7b9bbaa97: Mark the old BEAR route near Old Station
6a30b2fbd2999bc7b9bbaa98: Mark the old BEAR route near Scav bunker
6a30b2fbd2999bc7b9bbaa99: Mark the old BEAR route near Abandoned Village
6a30b2fbd2999bc7b9bbaab5 description: The old trails are open. That means people can move again. One of mine already did his part and left two sealed military weapon crates in the E2 bunkers on Reserve. He does not know your name, and you do not know his. That is how this works. Find the buried cache, recover both crates, and bring them to me sealed. If the wire is cut or one crate is missing, do not bother making excuses.
6a30b2fbd2999bc7b9bbaab5 note: Tony wants you to recover two sealed military weapon crates from the E2 bunkers on Reserve.
6a30b2fbd2999bc7b9bbaab5 startedMessageT: Reserve. E2 bunkers. Two sealed crates were left for us. Recover them and leave alive.
6a30b2fbd2999bc7b9bbaab5 successMessageT: Good. Both crates are sealed. That means my people can move product, and you can carry it without asking questions. I pulled a little loose steel from another box for you. Do not mistake it for generosity.
6a30b2fbd2999bc7b9bbaab5 failMessageText: A missing crate, a broken seal, or a dead courier. None of those help me.
6a30b2fbd2999bc7b9bbaab5 declinePlayerMe: Not going into E2 for crates yet.
6a30b2fbd2999bc7b9bbaab5 changeQuestMess: Tony wants both sealed crates recovered from the Reserve E2 bunkers.
6a30b2fbd2999bc7b9bbaab6: Locate the buried weapons cache in the Reserve E2 bunkers
6a30b2fbd2999bc7b9bbaab9: Hand over 2 sealed military weapon crates to Tony
6a30b2fbd2999bc7b9bbaaf1 description: Prapor's boys are shaving ammo before it reaches the books. They call it loss, damage, bad counts, whatever makes the paperwork sleep. I call it opportunity. One of those depot rats has a skimmed ammo cache inside K Building, K4, on Reserve. You are not taking it. You are marking it. My people will collect the ammo later, and Prapor's people will keep wondering where their missing rounds went.
6a30b2fbd2999bc7b9bbaaf1 note: Tony wants you to locate and mark a skimmed ammo cache in K Building, K4, on Reserve.
6a30b2fbd2999bc7b9bbaaf1 startedMessageT: Reserve. K Building, K4. Find the skimmed ammo cache and mark it. Do not haul boxes like a mule.
6a30b2fbd2999bc7b9bbaaf1 successMessageT: Good. The stash is marked. Prapor's rats stole from him, and now I steal from the rats. I pulled a few boxes of BP from the count before my people moved the rest. Do not ask where it came from.
6a30b2fbd2999bc7b9bbaaf1 failMessageText: You missed the stash or made too much noise near it. Depot rats scatter fast.
6a30b2fbd2999bc7b9bbaaf1 declinePlayerMe: Not touching Prapor's depot rats yet.
6a30b2fbd2999bc7b9bbaaf1 changeQuestMess: Tony wants the skimmed ammo cache in K Building, K4, marked for pickup.
6a30b2fbd2999bc7b9bbaaf2: Locate the skimmed ammo cache in K Building, K4
6a30b2fbd2999bc7b9bbaaf3: Mark the skimmed ammo cache for Tony
6a3210dbd2999bc7b9bbab06 description: Therapist throws away anything she cannot trust. That is why she sleeps better than most people in Tarkov, and why men like me still make money. Shoreline has a test batch that never made it back into clean hands: regular injectors, rejected batches, prototype stimulants, old labels, bad storage, maybe a few things Sanitar's people were smart enough not to touch. The case is in West 301 at the Health Resort. I will give you a key, but it is almost spent, so do not waste trips. Find the experimental stim case and bring it back sealed. Do not open it. Do not test anything.
6a3210dbd2999bc7b9bbab06 note: Tony wants you to recover an experimental stim case from room W301 at the Health Resort.
6a3210dbd2999bc7b9bbab06 startedMessageT: Shoreline. Health Resort west wing, room 301. Use the key, recover the experimental stim case, and bring it back sealed.
6a3210dbd2999bc7b9bbab06 successMessageT: Good. Case is sealed, labels are ugly, and half of this should probably never touch a living body. That means it will sell. Therapist would sort this by safety. I sort it by demand.
6a3210dbd2999bc7b9bbab06 failMessageText: You lost the case, opened it, wasted the key, or let Shoreline swallow you. None of that helps my business.
6a3210dbd2999bc7b9bbab06 changeQuestMess: Tony wants the experimental stim case recovered from West 301.
6a3210dbd2999bc7b9bbab07: Locate the experimental stim case in room W301 at the Health Resort
6a3210dbd2999bc7b9bbab0a: Hand over the experimental stim case to Tony
